review_text:
I have been here 3 weeks now , and planing a few more.
If you are wanting a peaceful beach location with a great community atmosphere this is for you very peaceful and dog friendly it is a stones throw from Minlaton which has all your needs covered .
The cabins are excellent, clean with everything you need . Lots of grassed sites some with water front views .
Rob , Darlene and Josh are the above and beyond type . NOTHING is to much , just love the long beach walks and a great jetty for fishing .

Possibly the most serene place in the known universe. Addictively peaceful. Absolutely loved it. Some of the facilities are all little older, although immaculately maintained, and the current managers are a delight. If you want a peaceful escape, this is the place.

I had to cancel my holiday I had booked as my grandson broke his collarbone and he couldn't go fishing. I paid 185 and it took near on a month for my refund from the so called Maitland Council  and they took out $37 for so called admin fees. I can't understand why they take so long to return the money but quick in taking it from us.
NOT HAPPY.

We had once again the best time in Port Rickaby.
We spent three days with our three grandchildren at the fabulous park. The weather was superb, the beach so safe to swim and play.
They loved riding with their bikes around the park and made friends with other children. The Delphines cruising past the jetty at sunset was spectacular.
Very friendly and helpful managers. Thank you for making our stay an unforgettable experience.
Caravan Park was the best
We had a great time in this park ! Amenities were clean & well looked after ! Owners very friendly & helpful ! Wouldn’t hesitate to stay again or recommend to others! AL & Les
Very friendly family that run this park. Really enjoyed our stay. Have a great kitchen. Recommend this park.

The location is amazing, any closer to the water and you would be paddling. The jetty is fantastic and the beach is great. New hosts are a breath of fresh air. Rob, Darlene and Josh couldn't do enough for you. Park is well equipped and maintained. We have been going at Easter for 25 years and it gets better and better. A serene relaxing experience. We took our van but the cabins are nice and clean.

It was a quiet little park at the time we went. The guy (Rob) that was managing it at the time was very helpful, would get anything when someone was going to Minlaton. Very nice guy. Place is very neat and tidy. Not much more at Port Rickaby.
Very nice caravan park, with lovely hosts. We felt very well welcomed and would always stay again!
Great little park. Will be back. Clean simple amenities. Walk to beach. Great views in the early morning and arvo. Short drive to nearest town (17kms).
Beautiful park well run by Rob and Darlene. Great location and nice sites.  Would be a bit manic when packed but was perfect for what we wanted

I had a lovely stay at Port Rickaby Caravan Park. The staff were super friendly and accommodating. The facilities were super clean even in the midst of summer which is super impressive. Would strongly recommend staying here!!!

This CP is Paradise. We stayed recently in Foreshore Cabin 2. What a view. New owners are doing an amazing job. Lovely people. Only negative is the BBQs in the park next to the Camp kitchen were not working.  And the communial BBQs were not cleaned during our 2 day stay. Apart from this minor negative a beautiful part of the Yorke.

We got a SA Tourism voucher and decided to come here. I must say the park is clean and grounds are very well looked after. We had a 5 night stay and have loved the whole atmosphere. We had a site right near the beach and jetty and it was absolutely beautiful and perfect. Highly recommend. Would definitely return.

We stayed here in our caravan in the off season. The park has a good view of the sea, and is in close proximity of both the beach and the jetty, along with other places of interest. We had lawn underfoot and the conveniences and park were both clean and tidy. This was topped of with friendly service. Located only 16km out of Minlaton.

Awesome park, a real hidden gem situated right on the beach - aqua water with some great body surf waves.
Cleanest cabin I've ever stayed in.
The kiosk is awesome, everything from steak sandwiches, fish and chips, hot dogs, etc right through to bread, milk, groceries, fishing gear. Everything's here. Great selection of drinks and ice-creams for those warm days.
The park itself is immaculate. The grounds, amenities building, laundry, are all very well kept. Park managers are very friendly and nothing is too much bother. I'll definitely be back!
This caravan park is tidy, clean and well looked after. We booked a powered site which was close to everything that a salt 'n peppered nomad seeks. Top marks to the owners!
Shame about the shop ,I've only ever seen it open once
Very windy, other than that nice quiet place
I recently stayed for a week on a powered site.   Very good park, amenities are clean, staff are friendly and helpful.   Location is beautiful.
Clean and neat cabin. Friendly service. Hot food at the reception.
Great site for caravan, quiet, nice walks ,toilets clean bit tired but okay been twice and will return
Quiet and green , clean showers and toilets.

Great little park. Staff were very accomodating. Loved the lawned areas and the ease in accessing jetty and beach from the park.
Just wondering if there has been any consideration given to adding a grab rail in the ladies toilet to assist people with mobility issues.
Great spot to relax take a walk and watch the sun set .

This is 1 of the best c/van parks we have been to. Close to the beach jetty .green grass super clean Lov the spot
Cheers Gavin Jules
The first night we stayed all power was out but the staff handled it well. Situated not far from the the beach would stay again

We called the Park and with no notice Amy was able to fit us in. Amy couldn’t have been more welcoming and accommodating.
Lovely quiet Park, Right on the water. Handy kiosk too. Fish off Jetty or enjoy a swim at gorgeous beach with tourquise clean waters.. Dog friendly too. Lovely walking track nearby. Ammenities were all clean and grounds very well maintained. Really enjoyed our stay!
Highly recommend.👍

Great spot to stay. Very windy in afternoon in Summer. SW. 30-40km hr.
Good fishing from jetty. Boat launch from beach. Caravan park is basic with kiosk and amenities. Grass sites nice.
Visited Port Rickaby for the day. Very clean and tidy town. Caravan Park looked well cared for.
Peaceful park

Great place, management very helful.
Very clean amenities block.
Aub & Jo

Only ever camped here for the past 2 years, never stayed in the cabins. Facilities are well maintained but quite small and cosy. Not a lot for the kids to do within the park other than ride bikes but the beach is a minute walk away and you always get an amazing sunset. Great kiosk if you don't want to cook and the Minlaton bakery is 15mins away.

Great little spot, close to Jetty. Has a reasonable BBQ area and handy fish cleaning table. Had trouble getting in after hours.

Just wished you felt welcomed though. Pretty tough to get a smile or hello when you walk in the office/kiosk.
Even when we asked for a cabin in the next few months, it wasn't like our enquiry was appreciated by the male 'owner ?'.

Pity, as it is a great little spot but we discovered the BBQ area I mentioned in the previous post is for the public. So are the toilets,  so if you wish to visit Pt. Rickaby, you can enjoy the Jetty &  beach too, without bothering to bother the Caravan Park owners.
Had   a cabin.   Very clean and main area roomy.   Park very well maintained.   Park owners friendly and helpful.   Park shop very good.
$45 a night for a gravel site, no black water dumping facilities and no grey water dumping facilities. Charge $10 extra per night per child but has no play equipment whatsoever. Overpriced.
Caravan Park  good management  sucks

We havnt been here for many years and have notice a decline in the park. Park is quite small but sites are a nice size. No tv reception unless you buy a cable at 30.00. Most parks we have been to lend you a cable. Fortunately we had our own 10 metre cable we got at Bunnings for 10.00. Beach is ok and sunsets lovely. Park has a shop with over cooked takeaway available to purchase. Very limited activities for kids with no park or play equiptment.Cover up as I have come home covered in sandfly Bites on my legs. Not for us.Better parks and places on YP

Like all the parks on the peninsula that are owned by the yorke peninsula council (most) they are a c standard and expensive as the council has always been anti progress and against further tourism.
Nowhere for waste water to go except on the ground. I went into the shop at 6.37 pm and was told we shut at 6.30 or we get to busy and sorry we shut 7 mins ago.  Toilets and showers are old and need replacement and as they dont have enough you need to use the public ones. Fantastic location but a poorly serviced one.

Perfect family holiday.
Very small caravan park, no play ground or pool, but being right by the beach wasn't missed by our 9 year old. Very sleepy "town",  lots of generational campers returning year after year for their summer holidays.  The only place we found to purchase food was the park kiosk, but Minlaton is only 15 minutes away has IGA for all supplies. Camp sites are huge, so lots of room for tent, gazebo, car, bikes, cricket pitch...Drinks at the jetty nightly for the gorgeous sunsets, great beach for water play with small children. Dolphins, stingrays, seal, fishes, pelicans all visit the beach. Fishing from the jetty they were catching lots of squid, and King George Whiting further out in boats. Park facilities are small, with only a handful of showers/toilets but we didn't have any issues. And the showers were great. Only 2 coin operated washing machines but we weren't on holidays to do laundry everyday!
We used Port Rickaby as a base to explore the Yorke Peninsula and loved every minute...well except for the wind but that's mother nature for you!
Couldn't be any closer 2the water which I love. Sent welcoming msg b4 we even left.
Great location! Close to the beach. Can beach launch your boat. Part of the Walk the Yorke pathway takes you to a second beach.

Clean cabins, double beds in both bedrooms, well equipped kitchen.  So close to the sea you can hear it all night. Great kiosk where take away food is available . Small well laid out park. This will be our second visit as the experience was so good last time.

Beach front caravan park, nice location
Little jetty good for fishing
Made me stay in a caravan. Wasn't allowed to see the beach and I was sent to a mental hospital after.
Powered sites for 7 days
